tasks.title: ADR-0023: Agent Registry Architecture
tasks.description: Capture decision: why Agent Registry matters, pillar design, alternatives considered
acceptance: ADR file exists with context + decision + consequences
Architectural diagram shows registry position in orchestration

tasks.title: Design agent.yaml Manifest Schema
tasks.description: Define JSON Schema for .aios/agent.yaml: name, version, inputs, outputs, dependencies, metadata
requirements: REQ-PKG-001: Manifest schema defined and documented
acceptance: Covers: name, version, inputs (ports), outputs (ports), dependencies (agent + engine)

tasks.title: Create @aios/agent-registry package scaffold
tasks.description: Initialize TypeScript package: src/, tests/, types/, README, tsconfig
requirements: REQ-PKG-002: Package structure ready for development
acceptance: README outlines core exports: parseManifest, validateAgent, listAgents, getAgentHealth

tasks.title: Implement AgentRegistry core class
tasks.description: Core logic: parse agent.yaml, validate against schema, resolve metadata, store local registry
requirements: REQ-REG-001: Registry can discover and validate agents
acceptance: AgentRegistry class with methods: parseManifest(path), validate(agent), listAgents(), getAgent(name)
Error handling for malformed manifests + missing dependencies

tasks.title: Implement metadata resolver (npm, git, local)
tasks.description: Fetch agent metadata from: npm registry, git repos, local filesystem
acceptance: git: clone + parse .aios/agent.yaml + get versions from tags
Caching (1h TTL) to avoid rate limits

tasks.title: CLI: aios list-agents command
tasks.description: CLI command to list agents: built-in, community, local. Filters by tag, maintainer, health-score
acceptance: Output: table with agent, version, maintainer, description, health-score
JSON output mode: aios list-agents --json > agents.json

tasks.title: MCP: aios_list_agents tool
tasks.description: MCP protocol implementation for agent discovery (Cursor/IDE integration)
acceptance: Returns JSON schema: { agents: [...{name, version, description, tags}] }
Callable from Cursor/Claude via MCP stdio

tasks.title: Local registry persistence
tasks.description: Persist discovered agents to .aios/agents.registry.json with update strategy
acceptance: Update on: list-agents, refresh command, startup scan
Merge strategy: local > npm > git (priority order)

tasks.title: Manifest validator (JSON Schema + rules)
tasks.description: Validate agent.yaml against schema + runtime rules (no circular deps, valid semver, etc.)
acceptance: Detects: missing fields, invalid semver, circular dependencies, unknown engine refs

tasks.title: npm scaffolder: npm create @aios/agent
tasks.description: Create npm init template for agents. Generates folder + manifest + src/index.ts
acceptance: Generated: my-security-agent/ with agent.yaml, src/, tests/, README
npm test works out of box

tasks.title: Dependency resolver (multi-level graph)
tasks.description: Resolve agent → agent → engine dependency graph. Detect conflicts, cycle detection
acceptance: Warns on missing or stale deps

tasks.title: Publish @aios/agent-template on npm
tasks.description: Create + publish npm package for agent template (scaffolder consumer)
acceptance: Example agents in template/ folder (security, docs, qa)

tasks.title: Documentation: Writing an Agent
tasks.description: Guide: architecture, I/O contract, testing, debugging, manifest format
acceptance: Sections: concept, anatomy, I/O ports, testing, debugging, manifest

tasks.title: Agent scaffolder integration tests
tasks.description: Test suite: scaffold → validate → run tests → execute simple orchestration
acceptance: Integration test: npm create → npm test → aios list-agents shows new agent
Dependency resolution: test agent that depends on built-in agent
CI: test passes on 4 template variations (simple/medium/complex/with-deps)

tasks.title: Add recordAgentExecution hook to Orchestration Engine
tasks.description: Instrumentation: record every agent execution with metadata (outcome, duration, cost)
acceptance: recordAgentExecution(agent_name, version, input_tokens, output_tokens, outcome, duration_ms)

tasks.title: Metrics schema: kind: agent.execution
tasks.description: Define metrics event structure for .aios/metrics/events.jsonl (agent execution lines)
acceptance: Event schema: { timestamp, kind: 'agent.execution', agent_name, version, outcome, duration_ms, cost_usd, input_tokens, output_tokens }
Recorded to .aios/metrics/events.jsonl (JSONL format, one event per line)
Backwards compat: existing metric kinds unchanged

tasks.title: Health-score calculator
tasks.description: Compute health-score from: success-rate (70%), recency (20%), adoption (10%)
acceptance: Recency: penalize agents not run in 7+ days

tasks.title: Console card: Agent Catalog
tasks.description: UI: list agents with trending, top-used, health visualization
acceptance: Console health page includes 'Agent Catalog' section
Columns: agent name, version, health-score, executions (7d), success-rate, avg-cost

tasks.title: Adoption curve metrics
tasks.description: Time-series: executions per day per agent (trend detection for Phase 6)
acceptance: Query: adoption trend (last 30d) for each agent
Console: graph on Agent Catalog card (trending agents)

tasks.title: Integration: metrics → console dashboard
tasks.description: Pipe metrics.jsonl → health snapshot → UI rendering (daily refresh)
requirements: REQ-UI-002: Live dashboard with real metrics
acceptance: Console loads agents.json (daily updated from metrics)
Agent Catalog shows real execution data

tasks.title: E2E test: record + view agent execution
tasks.description: Integration test: run orchestration → recordAgentExecution fires → metrics appear in console
acceptance: Assertion: metrics.jsonl has event with agent name, outcome, cost
Assertion: console dashboard reflects new execution

tasks.title: Backwards-compat: wrap engines/* agents with observability
tasks.description: Auto-instrument built-in agents (Architecture, AppSec, Docs, QA) to emit metrics
acceptance: Built-in agents wrapped transparently (no code changes)
Metrics recorded with engine name as agent_name

tasks.title: GitHub Actions: detect aios-agent topic
tasks.description: Workflow: scan GitHub repos with topic 'aios-agent', extract metadata, ingest
acceptance: Extracts: repo owner, name, description, homepage, topics
Posts to ingestion service (webhook or batch upload)

tasks.title: Async registry ingestion service
tasks.description: Service: receive agent metadata from GitHub workflow, validate, store in central registry
acceptance: Validate: agent.yaml exists + parseable + no malware signatures

tasks.title: Abuse detection heuristics
tasks.description: Rules: detect spam, stale repos, duplicate names, suspicious permissions
acceptance: Rule: repo inactive >6 months → flag 'stale', hide from catalog
Rule: agent name duplicate → reject (needs owner negotiation)
Rule: agent.yaml requests dangerous permissions → flag 'review'
Log all flags for manual review (first month)

tasks.title: Documentation: Publish Your Agent
tasks.description: Guide: step-by-step to publish community agent (GitHub topic, manifest, submit)
acceptance: Steps: 1) scaffold, 2) develop+test, 3) tag aios-agent, 4) add topic on GitHub, 5) wait for weekly ingest
FAQ: why denied? how to appeal? how to update?

tasks.title: First community agent ingestion (manual test)
tasks.description: Test: publish dummy community agent → GitHub Actions detects → ingestion succeeds → appears in catalog
acceptance: Create test repo with aios-agent topic + valid manifest
Assert: agent appears in aios list-agents

tasks.title: Registry validation gate
tasks.description: Review: enforce manifest schema compliance before acceptance
acceptance: Gate: agents with validation errors → rejected + email maintainer
Validation includes: schema, abuse heuristics, dependency check

tasks.title: Migrate built-in agents to registry
tasks.description: Move Architecture, AppSec, Docs, QA agents from engines/* → packages/agents/* with agent.yaml
acceptance: aios list-agents shows all 4 as built-in (marked 'official')
No breaking changes to agent I/O

tasks.title: Backwards compatibility wrapper
tasks.description: Shim: legacy engines/* agent references still work (transparent migration)
acceptance: Migration guide for users (no urgent action needed)

tasks.title: Cross-workspace discovery
tasks.description: Multi-repo context: agents from workspace A discoverable in workspace B
acceptance: workspace-registry resolves agent scope (workspace A:agent X)
aios list-agents --workspace=all shows agents from all workspaces

tasks.title: Companion integration: MCP agent discovery
tasks.description: Companion repo can query AIOS agents via MCP (aios_list_agents, aios_get_agent_docs)
acceptance: Companion uses to show 'Available agents' in conversation

tasks.title: End-to-end: scaffold → register → discover → execute
tasks.description: Integration test: new agent flow (scaffold → publish locally → list → run orchestration)
acceptance: Test flow: npm create agent → manifest filled → aios list-agents → use in orchestration

tasks.title: CHANGELOG: Phase 5 release notes
tasks.description: Document: features, breaking changes, migration guide (if any), credits
acceptance: Sections: Added (agents, registry, metrics), Changed (none major), Fixed (if any)

tasks.title: Completeness gate + Phase 5 sign-off
tasks.description: Quality gate: all REQ-* mapped to tasks, tests passing, docs complete, no orphans
acceptance: All REQ-* requirements traced to tasks
